mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal consumption expenditures signal robust aggregate demand, which boosts corporate sales and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window or 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive when RSAFS surpasses its 12-month average (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds a critical threshold, the market anticipates a tightening cycle that raises discount rates and reduces equity valuations, causing negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: In periods where CPIAUCSL YoY > 3%, the median 60-day SPX return is not negative (sign test p > 0.10) or rank IC fails to be negative at any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the unemployment rate and increases in nonfarm payrolls indicate a strengthening labor market, which boosts disposable income and corporate profits, driving equity prices higher over a three-week horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (UNRATE change * PAYEMS change) fails to show a statistically significant positive coefficient (p < 0.05) in a linear regression of 20-day SPX returns over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp declines in industrial production, when occurring in a recessionary context, signal contraction in output and earnings, leading to negative weekly equity returns as the market reprices downside risk.
failure_condition: During NBER recessions, the triggered signal (INDPRO decline) does not predict negative 5-day returns with at least 60% accuracy, or the false positive rate exceeds 50% in non-recessionary declines.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts rising above a critical level signal a self-reinforcing cycle of construction investment, employment, and consumption, which expands aggregate demand and lifts equity markets over a one-quarter horizon.
failure_condition: In months where HOUST exceeds 1200, the subsequent 60-day SPX return is not significantly positive (mean < 0, t-test one-sided p > 0.05) over multiple cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below CPI inflation, real interest rates are negative, which discourages savings and pushes capital into equities, boosting forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, the median 20-day SPX return is not greater than the unconditional median (Wilcoxon rank-sum test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high unemployment directly depresses household incomes and consumer confidence, leading to lower spending and weaker economic activity, which translates into negative weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: The correlation between weekly changes in UNRATE and subsequent 5-day SPX returns is not statistically different from zero (p > 0.10) or the Rank IC is below 0.02 in a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth in the initial phase of a post-recession recovery reflects pent-up demand and re-accelerating activity, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In quarters following a trough with GDP growth above 3%, the forward 60-day SPX return is not positive in at least 70% of observations, or the average return is not statistically above the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.10).
